name: United Arab Emirates (presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the falcon and its parchment — heritage and authority

description: A green field charged centrally with a golden falcon with outstretched wings, holding a parchment inscribed in Arabic with the name of the state and bearing on its breast a circular shield reproducing the UAE flag, the whole encircled by seven five-pointed stars representing the seven emirates.
history: Adopted in 2008, replacing the 1973 presidential standard that had been used since the federation's founding. The falcon is a long-standing emblem of authority in the Gulf; the seven stars represent the seven federating emirates.

features.role: golden falcon with outstretched wings, holding a parchment with the state's name in Arabic and bearing a roundel of the UAE flag on its breast
the seven emirates, encircling the falcon
